##NAMES=Pleysteinite
##RRUFFID=R250062
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)K]Mn_2_Al_3_(PO_4_)_4_F_2_(H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Hagendorf Süd mine, Oberpfalz, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Both isolated and aggregates of colourless, stubby prisms in association with nordgauite, fluellite, rockbridgeite, pyrite and columbite. Type sample, described in Grey et al (2023), with a revised structure and symmetry from Rewitzer et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250062
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)_0.50_K_0.50_]_2_(Mn_1.20_Mg_0.49_Fe^2+^_0.27_Zn_0.05_)(Al_1.63_Fe^3+^_0.20_Ti^4+^_0.19_)(Al_0.56_Ti^4+^_0.44_)(PO_4_)_4.02_[F_0.58_O_0.31_(OH)_0.11_]_2_(H_2_O)_10_·3.92H_2_O
